name: FAI Meta-Agent
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Meta-agent intelligence — GPT-4o for high-level reasoning: play selection from 100-play catalog based on natural language requirements, infrastructure planning and Bicep template generation, multi-step orchestration planning with dependency resolution; GPT-4o-mini for sub-tasks: requirement parsing, documentation generation, status summarization, user communication; structured output for deterministic play configuration and infra provisioning commands

dev.note: ~150K tokens/day, gpt-4o for reasoning + gpt-4o-mini for sub-tasks

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput, <100ms inference for real-time orchestration

services.name: MCP Server
services.purpose: Tool orchestration layer — 25+ FAI MCP tools for play discovery (semantic search, filtering, comparison), architecture generation (Mermaid diagrams, service role tables), cost estimation (per-play pricing at dev/prod/enterprise scale), knowledge base queries (modules, glossary, best practices), evaluation scoring (quality gates, threshold checks), and infrastructure provisioning commands; MCP protocol enables the meta-agent to compose any combination of tools dynamically based on user intent

prod.note: 2 vCPU, 4GB, always-ready for sub-second tool response

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Meta-agent runtime — orchestration engine managing multi-step workflows (requirement analysis → play selection → architecture design → infra provisioning → deployment → evaluation), session management for long-running provisioning tasks, WebSocket interface for real-time status streaming, sub-agent coordination for parallel infrastructure deployments, retry and rollback management for failed provisioning steps

prod.note: 4 vCPU, 8GB, min 3 replicas, persistent WebSocket sessions

enterprise.note: 8 vCPU, 16GB, multi-region, 10+ replicas, dedicated orchestration pool

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Orchestration state — active session state machines (requirement → planning → provisioning → deploying → evaluating → complete), provisioned infrastructure inventory, play execution history with full audit trail, user preference profiles, cost tracking per orchestration session, rollback checkpoints, cached play analysis results for faster re-runs

prod.note: Dedicated throughput, sub-10ms state machine transitions

services.name: Azure AI Search
services.purpose: Play knowledge retrieval — semantic search across 100 play architectures, 830+ primitives, 17 knowledge modules, and 200+ glossary terms; hybrid search (keyword + vector) for precise play matching against user requirements; faceted filtering by complexity, services, industry, WAF pillars; powers the meta-agent's contextual understanding of the entire FAI ecosystem

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Orchestration secrets — Azure OpenAI API keys, infrastructure provisioning credentials (service principal for Bicep deployments), MCP tool authentication tokens, AI Search admin keys, user session encryption keys, rollback authorization tokens

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Meta-agent KPIs — end-to-end orchestration latency (requirement to production), play selection accuracy (user satisfaction with recommendations), provisioning success rate, tool invocation patterns, cost per orchestration session, user engagement and retention metrics

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for initial requirement parsing and play filtering (80% of queries match well-known patterns) — reserve GPT-4o for complex multi-play compositions, ambiguous requirements, and architecture reasoning; tiered model routing saves 65% on inference costs

Cache play analysis results in Cosmos DB — when users request similar architectures, serve cached play recommendations and cost estimates instead of re-computing; cache hit rates of 40-60% are typical for common enterprise patterns like 'build a RAG chatbot' or 'deploy an AI gateway'

Implement progressive orchestration — generate architecture plan first (cheap) and require user confirmation before triggering infrastructure provisioning (expensive); prevents wasted provisioning costs from misunderstood requirements

Use AI Search free tier for development — the FAI knowledge base fits within the 50MB free index limit; only upgrade to Standard when production traffic requires semantic ranker and higher query throughput

Schedule infrastructure teardown for dev/test environments — meta-agent provisions full stacks for testing that are often forgotten; implement automatic teardown after 72 hours of inactivity to prevent runaway infrastructure costs

Implement tool call batching in MCP — combine related tool calls (play detail + cost estimate + architecture diagram) into single batched requests instead of sequential calls; reduces Container Apps compute time and improves response latency by 40%
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
